1._: Yarem comes up the southern road to the meeting of the ways, a lean weathered stranger in road-worn hides whose skin and manner are not of this country, and halts before the gather-stone as is right among his people, to be seen before he goes down. Two others wait at the crossroads, come up off other roads; he knows the word they will have for him in their eyes before they have his name — barab, outlander. He reads the new marks scratched into the stone the way he reads the sky, and on the cold off the high ridges he catches it: the same wrong he once fled.

content._: yarem's account at Thousand Valleys, beat by beat.
